title: Writing: The Past Tense Challenge
learningGoal: Construct grammatically accurate sentences in the past tense, correctly choosing between intransitive subject-matching and transitive object-matching ('ne' rule).
instructions: This is the ultimate test of your Hindi grammar skills. You must translate sentences into the past tense. FIRST, ask yourself: 'Does this action have an object?'. If YES, you must use 'ne' and match the verb to the object. If NO, do not use 'ne'. ASSUME YOU ARE A MALE SPEAKER.
writingPrompt:
Translate the following sentences into Hindi (Assume you are a MALE speaker):
1. I went home. (Go = Jaa. Intransitive.)
2. I drank water. (Drink = Pee. Water = Paani [Masc]. Transitive.)
3. I read a book. (Read = Padh. Book = Kitaab [Fem]. Transitive.)
4. He slept. (Sleep = So. Intransitive.)
5. What did you do? (Casual 'You'. Do = Kar. Transitive.)
promptGoal: Applying ergative vs absolutive past tense rules.
referenceHints: Sentence 1: 'I home went.' (No 'ne' because go is intransitive)
Sentence 2: 'I-ne water drank.' (Water is masc, so verb is piya)
Sentence 3: 'I-ne book read.' (Book is fem, so verb is padhi)
Sentence 4: 'He slept.' (No 'ne' because sleep is intransitive)

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: मैं घर गया। मैंने पानी पिया। मैंने किताब पढ़ी। वह सो गया। तुमने क्या किया?
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Main ghar gaya. Maine paani piya. Maine kitaab padhi. Woh so gaya. Tumne kya kiya?
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: I went home. I drank water. I read a book. He slept. What did you do?
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect execution. The student correctly avoided 'ne' for 'gaya' and 'so gaya'. The student used 'Maine' and matched 'piya' to masculine water, and 'padhi' to feminine book.

evaluationCriteria.description: Must use 'Main' (not Maine) for 'gaya'. Must use 'Woh' for 'so gaya'.
Must use 'Maine' for drinking and reading, and 'Tumne' for doing.
Must use 'piya' (masc) for water and 'padhi' (fem) for book. If 'padha' is used for book, score drops significantly.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: मैं घर गया। मैंने पानी पिया। मैंने किताब पढ़ी। वह सो गया। तुमने क्या किया?
acceptableAlternates: Main ghar gaya. Maine paani piya. Maine kitaab padhi. Woh so gaya. Tumne kya kiya?
main ghar gaya. maine paani piya. maine kitaab padhi. woh so gaya. tumne kya kiya?
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Accepts transliteration. Extremely strict check on 'ne' and gender.

criteria.name: Maine Gaya Error
criteria.description: If user writes 'Maine gaya' or 'Maine ghar gaya', the score for that sentence is 0.

criteria.name: Object Gender Error
criteria.description: If user writes 'Maine kitaab padha', they fail the sentence because book is feminine.

feedbackRules.onPass: Incredible! You have passed the hardest grammar test in the Hindi language.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review Section 2 and 4. Remember: Intransitive (go, sleep) = NO NE. Transitive (eat, drink, read) = NE + MATCH OBJECT.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Make sure you don't use 'ne' on verbs that don't take an object (like 'to go'). And remember, 'Kitaab' is feminine, so it is 'padhi'.

hintText: Sentence 3 is the trickiest. Even though you are a male speaker, you MUST write 'padhi' because the book you read is feminine.
commonMistakes: Writing 'Maine kitaab padha' (Making the verb match the male speaker instead of the feminine book).
